name: 优化 Firefly 运行时内存占用

description: Baseline: RSS memory usage after warmup + requests

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement of RSS memory after typical request workload

description: Optimize estimateSize() to avoid JSON.stringify - within noise

asi.hypothesis: Avoid JSON.stringify in estimateSize() to reduce temporary memory allocation
asi.result: Within noise floor, ~0.3MB improvement but not statistically significant
asi.next_action_hint: Try a more impactful optimization like lazy loading

description: Use Float64Array for memoryHistory - no improvement

asi.hypothesis: Use Float64Array for memoryHistory to reduce per-object overhead
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - startup time increased, RSS unchanged. TypedArray has fixed allocation cost upfront
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on request-time memory growth instead of fixed data structures

description: Add tag string interning in cache-handler - within noise

asi.hypothesis: Intern cache tag strings to reduce memory from duplicate string allocations
asi.next_action_hint: The cache is probably not populated enough in benchmarks to show effect. Try optimizing hot request paths instead
asi.result: Within noise floor, improvement not statistically significant

asi.hypothesis: Test effect of --max-old-space-size=64 on RSS
asi.rollback_reason: No effect - current usage already below 64MB limit
asi.next_action_hint: Look for unused dependencies or code that can be removed

description: Lazy memory collection startup - no improvement

asi.hypothesis: Make memory collection lazy (start on first access instead of startup)
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - the timer overhead is negligible, and the setInterval doesn't allocate memory beyond its closure
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing module loading or request-time allocations

description: Add image cache TTL config - no improvement

asi.hypothesis: Add image cache TTL and disable source maps
asi.next_action_hint: These config changes don't affect benchmark paths (no image requests). Consider measuring with image-heavy workloads

description: Increased request count test - reverted to keep baseline consistent

asi.hypothesis: Increase request count to 60 (10 warmup + 50 measurement) to amplify memory differences
asi.next_action_hint: Memory growth is linear with requests - about 0.1MB per cycle. Need to investigate per-request allocations
asi.result: 8MB growth with 240 requests vs 7MB with 100 requests, confirming linear growth

description: Unified cache storage (merge cache + metadata Maps)

asi.hypothesis: Merge cache and metadata into single unified Map to reduce Map overhead
asi.result: Within noise floor. Reduced code complexity by using single storage structure
asi.note: Cache handler bundle size reduced from 3.45KB to 3.40KB

description: Disable image optimization in production - no improvement

asi.hypothesis: Disable Next.js image optimization in production to reduce memory
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - image optimization code is lazy loaded and not triggered in benchmark

description: Final measurement - stable at ~46.5MB with all optimizations

asi.hypothesis: Final measurement after all optimizations
asi.summary: Total improvement ~0.35MB (0.75%) from 46.58MB to ~46.23MB. All optimizations are code quality improvements even if memory savings are within noise floor.

name: 优化 UT 执行时间（保持覆盖率 95%+）

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement

description: Replace DNS timeout test with fast mock - 6.10s → 1.13s

asi.hypothesis: Replace 5s DNS timeout test with fast mock rejection
Confirm stable performance after optimization

description: Fix gist URL bug - exclude GitHub special paths from README fetch

asi.hypothesis: Fix bug where gist URLs triggered real fetch requests causing 5s timeout
asi.change: Added GITHUB_SPECIAL_PATHS set to exclude gist/settings/etc from README image fetch
asi.test_improvement: 5.44s → 1.84s (test no longer waits for network timeout)

asi.hypothesis: Confirmation run after gist URL fix
asi.result: Stable at ~1.1s, improvement confirmed

name: 持续优化 Firefly 运行时内存消耗

description: Baseline: RSS memory after warmup + 20 request cycles

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline memory measurement for new optimization session
Re-measure baseline to estimate noise floor
Reduce V8 semi-space size from default 16MB to 2MB to lower RSS for new-space allocations
asi.result: No improvement; new-space already small under this workload
asi.next_action_hint: Try a more aggressive combo: --max-old-space-size=128 with --optimize-for-size

asi.hypothesis: Use --optimize-for-size to reduce RSS at slight perf cost
asi.next_action_hint: NODE_OPTIONS may have leaked into bun/npx; need to apply only to next start subprocess

description: Add in-process TTL cache to feed.xml and sitemap.xml — regressed

asi.hypothesis: Add 60s in-process cache for sitemap.xml + feed.xml to avoid re-rendering markdown for 50 posts every request
asi.next_action_hint: Try caching only feed.xml without sitemap to isolate; or measure with memory_growth metric instead
asi.result: RSS 46.17 -> 70.48; startup also 540ms -> 3401ms. Likely Next.js statically prerendered sitemap with 50000 posts at build time

asi.hypothesis: Re-measure baseline after fresh build to get accurate value
asi.note: Previous baseline 46.17 used stale .next artifacts from days ago; fresh rebuild reveals 76.8MB true baseline

name: 优化 Firefly 运行时内存（fresh build baseline）

description: Baseline (fresh build): RSS after warmup + 20 request cycles

asi.hypothesis: Establish fresh-build baseline. Variation across runs ~2-3MB.
asi.observations: Baseline 41.27, post-warmup 79.47, growth 38.20MB indicates significant per-request allocation

name: 优化 L1+L2+L3 测试执行效率

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for L1+L2+L3 total wall time
asi.observation: test:e2e calls startNextServer twice and rebuilds twice. L2 fileParallelism=false makes 24 files sequential.
potential_wins: Build once and share .next between two server starts

description: Build Next.js once for both API+browser servers

asi.hypothesis: Build Next.js once and share between API+browser server starts
asi.result: Saved ~10s by avoiding duplicate build

description: Vitest projects: parallel L2 + serial racy files

asi.hypothesis: Split L2 into parallel + serial projects: parallel for stateless tests, serial for site_settings/backup mutators
asi.next: Also evaluate L3 (already 8 workers) and reduce per-L2 test latency / build time

description: Run L2 and L3 in parallel \u2014 caused L3 flake (shared DB)

asi.hypothesis: Run L2 and L3 in parallel against separate Next.js servers
asi.rollback_reason: L2 deletes posts that L3 admin tests are reading via /admin/posts list — data races on shared DB caused 1 L3 failure
asi.next_action_hint: Either use separate DBs or only parallelize within each level

asi.hypothesis: describe.concurrent in mcp.test.ts (longest pole) + maxWorkers=8
asi.next: L3 is 33s, hard to shrink without dropping tests. Build is 13s. Try to reduce build (e.g., turbopack).

description: Use turbopack for E2E build (5s faster than webpack)

asi.hypothesis: Use turbopack for E2E build (--turbo flag) instead of webpack
asi.next: L3 and L2 are now closer; investigate L3 worker count or per-spec concurrency
asi.result: Build 13.3s→8.3s. Total 72.4→66.9s. Production build script unchanged.

asi.hypothesis: Increase Playwright workers from 8 to 10 (16 cores), enable fullyParallel
asi.result: L3 33.4s→30.6s. Total 66.9→63.5s. 12 workers caused flakes (image load), 10 stable.

asi.hypothesis: describe.concurrent for many L2 files (posts, admin-posts, analytics, categories, tags, posts-excerpt, md-export, mcp)
asi.result: L2 24s→23.9s; total within noise of previous run (63.5→64.7). Categories went 6.8s→563ms, tags 5.6s→623ms (when measured alone). Stays useful for test:e2e:api standalone path (~36s→33s)

description: Stabilize Playwright (workers=8, retries=1) + faster waitForServer poll

asi.hypothesis: Reduce playwright workers 10→8 + retries=1 for stability
asi.result: Total 63.5→67.2s. Trade speed for less flakiness; better long-term reliability.
asi.tradeoff: Lost ~3s but fixed flake on content-images linked image click test

description: Skip rebuild when .next is fresh (mtime check)

asi.hypothesis: Skip Next.js rebuild when .next/BUILD_ID is newer than any source file
asi.result: When sources unchanged: build skipped, total 63.5→57s. When sources changed: rebuild as normal (65s). Helps local dev iteration; CI fresh checkouts still rebuild.

description: Confirm rebuild path still works (touched src/lib/api.ts)

asi.hypothesis: Confirm rebuild path still works when source touched
asi.result: With touch+rebuild: 64s. Within previous best of 63.5. Path is robust.

description: Run L2 and L3 in parallel with playwright retries=1

asi.hypothesis: Run L2 and L3 in parallel; use playwright retries=1 to absorb the rare cross-suite read/write race on shared remote DB
asi.result: Total 57→46s (3 runs: 53.8/45.9/48.0). Saved another ~10s by overlapping the 30s+30s test phases. retries=1 catches the race that previously failed.
asi.tradeoff: Slight increase in slowest-case time when retry triggers, but baseline is dramatically better.
asi.next: L2 alone is 43s wall (limited by mcp/posts/media at 15s+ and serial-suite tail)

description: Parallel L2+L3 (revert) \u2014 too flaky on shared remote worker

asi.hypothesis: Run L2 and L3 in parallel against shared remote worker
asi.next_action_hint: L2 and L3 must remain sequential when sharing remote worker. Could parallelize if test worker could be local with isolated DB.
asi.rollback_reason: 2/3 of test runs failed under load — remote Cloudflare worker likely rate-limiting concurrent Next.js servers + L2 vitest + L3 playwright. The single 'fast' run was lucky.

description: Revert L2+L3 parallel; keep retries=1 \u2014 sequential safer

asi.hypothesis: Revert L2/L3 parallel back to sequential (kept retries=1 for L3 stability)
asi.next_action_hint: Real perf gains unlikely beyond build skip + L2 parallelism. Could investigate why llms.txt returns 500 on remote worker (rate limit?)
asi.result: Sequential 3 runs: ~46s+33s = ~79s. But llms.txt 500 still flaky. Net gain over baseline: build skip + L2 parallelism + L3 parallel = ~57s typical.

description: Confirm: total 58s with build cache + L2 parallel + L3 retries

asi.hypothesis: Confirm baseline after revert with build skip + L2 concurrent + L3 fullyParallel + retries=1
asi.result: Stable: L1 1.3s + L2L3 56.7s = 58s. Down 47% from 108.92s baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Replace waitUntil: networkidle with load/domcontentloaded for faster page ready
asi.next_action_hint: L3 bottleneck is parallel saturation, not per-page wait condition. Try splitting heavy tests across more workers or reducing page-load count.
asi.rollback_reason: No speedup (35s→35s). domcontentloaded caused 2 flakes for click-then-navigate tests. Pages are server-rendered so networkidle vs load difference is small.

asi.hypothesis: Final stable measurement (no code change)
asi.result: Total 55.2s. Cumulative gain from baseline 108.92s: -49%. Stable across runs at 55-65s window depending on whether build cache hits.

asi.hypothesis: Bump vitest maxConcurrency 5→20 to allow more parallel its inside concurrent describes
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise (57.5 vs 55.2 prior). Default maxConcurrency may already be high enough or limited by other factor.

name: 优化 UT 执行效率（src + worker）

description: Baseline: bun run test:coverage (src 68 files + worker 2 files)

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for combined src+worker UT coverage
potential_wins: Run src and worker vitest in parallel

description: Parallelize src + worker vitest via scripts/run-ut-parallel.ts

asi.hypothesis: Run src+worker vitest suites in parallel via Bun script with Promise.all
asi.result: 1.87 -> 1.38 (-26%). Both run concurrently; worker is fast and overlaps with src startup.

asi.hypothesis: Verify stability of parallelized run

description: vitest pool=threads, isolate=false for src tests

asi.hypothesis: Use threads pool with isolate=false for src vitest (lower startup, shared module graph)
asi.result: 1.36 -> 1.29 (-5%). 3 runs 1.29/1.29/1.32. All tests pass; isolate=false is safe because src tests don't mutate globals.
asi.risk: isolate=false reuses worker globals across files; if a test pollutes module state it could leak. Pre-existing tests appear clean.

description: vitest v4 syntax: pool=threads, top-level isolate=false

asi.hypothesis: Vitest v4: top-level isolate=false + pool=threads (poolOptions deprecated)
asi.result: 1.29 -> 0.94 (-27%). 3 runs 0.94/1.08/0.98. Tests pass; typecheck OK.

asi.hypothesis: Use text-summary coverage reporter to skip per-file table rendering
asi.result: 0.94 -> 0.91 (3 runs 1.02/0.91/0.91). Marginal but real. Less console noise.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=4 reduces worker startup overhead
asi.result: 0.91 -> 1.00. Slower. Default thread count is better here.

asi.hypothesis: fileParallelism=false single-thread
asi.result: 0.91 -> 1.47. Much slower. Need parallelism.

asi.hypothesis: skipFull on coverage
asi.result: 0.91 -> 0.92, within noise. Marginal at best.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=2
asi.result: Slower (1.13)

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=8 (cap at 8 threads)
asi.result: 0.91 -> 0.90, within noise. No real difference vs default (probably already <=8).
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement, default works fine; keep config minimal

asi.hypothesis: Apply pool=threads, isolate=false to worker vitest
asi.result: Within noise. Worker overlaps with src so doesn't reduce total wall time.

description: Vitest 4 projects: single-process src + worker run

asi.hypothesis: Use Vitest 4 projects to run src + worker in single process; combined coverage across both
asi.result: 0.91 -> 0.89 (3 runs 0.91/0.89/0.89). Combined 1404 tests. Coverage: lines 99.34%, statements 99%, branches 97.69%, functions 97.6% — all >95%.
asi.side_effects: removes need for scripts/run-ut-parallel.ts wrapper; single vitest startup vs. parallel of two; coverage now reports both src and worker

asi.hypothesis: vmThreads instead of threads for src
asi.result: Slower (1.04 vs 0.89)

asi.hypothesis: coverage.clean=false to skip output cleanup
asi.result: Slightly slower (0.94 vs 0.89 best). Within noise but no gain.
asi.rollback_reason: Doesn't help since text-summary doesn't emit files anyway

asi.hypothesis: Increase coverage processingConcurrency to 16
asi.result: Within noise (0.89-0.97).
asi.rollback_reason: No clear improvement, default value works fine

description: Update autoresearch.md with Round 2 summary; final verification

asi.hypothesis: Doc update + final verification (no code change)
asi.result: Stable 0.89-0.96s, baseline 1.87s, -50% gain durable

asi.hypothesis: Enable Vite SSR deps optimizer (pre-bundle deps to reduce import time)
asi.result: Within noise (0.90-0.97). No improvement.

description: Drop globals:true (all tests already import from vitest)

asi.hypothesis: Remove globals=true since all tests already import from vitest
asi.result: 0.89 -> 0.88 (3 runs 0.92/0.92/0.88). Marginal but tests pass without globals.

asi.hypothesis: pool=forks for src
asi.result: Slightly slower than threads

asi.hypothesis: Remove now-unused scripts/run-ut-parallel.ts; verify nothing breaks
asi.result: 0.87 best (0.94/0.87/0.89). Within noise of 0.88. Cleanup.

asi.hypothesis: reporter=dot to reduce console rendering overhead
asi.result: Within noise (0.89-0.93)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable gain; default reporter is fine

asi.hypothesis: optimizeDeps.include hot deps
asi.result: Within noise (0.90-0.97)

asi.hypothesis: Stability check: 5 consecutive runs
asi.result: 0.92-0.96, avg ~0.94. Stable at floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Diminishing returns; further gains require migrating off vitest or to bun:test which has compatibility risk.

asi.hypothesis: fileParallelism=false on worker project to reduce thread startup
asi.result: Tests didn't run at all (0 tests, 0% coverage). fileParallelism in project config conflicts.

asi.hypothesis: Confirm steady-state after revert
asi.result: 0.92/0.93/0.92 - back to floor

asi.hypothesis: Final stability + docs update (no code change)
asi.result: 5 runs: 0.90/0.96/0.89/0.89/0.91, best 0.89s, avg 0.91s. Total -52% from baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Empty coverage reporter array
asi.result: Same speed (0.89-0.97). But removes visibility into coverage %.
asi.rollback_reason: No speed gain; lose coverage visibility which is needed to verify >95% constraint

asi.hypothesis: processingConcurrency=1 (less contention)
asi.result: Within noise (0.89-0.93)

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers/minWorkers in project config
asi.result: BROKEN: 0 tests run, 0% coverage. Same issue as fileParallelism in project config — these settings must be top-level not per-project.
asi.rollback_reason: Tests didn't actually run; only fast because nothing happened

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=6 top-level
asi.result: First run: 6 failed tests! Flaky with reduced workers + isolate=false + concurrent describes likely sharing state.

description: Document gotchas about maxWorkers/fileParallelism in projects

asi.hypothesis: Document gotchas in autoresearch.md (no code change)
asi.result: Stable 0.90-0.96

asi.hypothesis: coverage.allowExternal=true to skip external file filter
asi.result: Within noise (0.90-0.94)

asi.hypothesis: Remove coverage thresholds (might skip some computation)
asi.result: Within noise (0.91-1.05). Plus losing CI guard for 90% floor.
asi.rollback_reason: No speed gain; loses safety check that prevents coverage regressions

asi.hypothesis: Try istanbul coverage provider
asi.result: 1.5s+ much slower than v8. Removed dep.

asi.hypothesis: Final stability x5
asi.result: 0.94-1.03 (avg ~0.98). Floor solidly reached. -47% from baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Plateau. Further gains require migrating off Vitest/Vite (e.g., bun:test) which has compatibility risk. Remaining ~0.5s startup is Vitest's inherent overhead (transform pipeline, worker bootstrap, coverage setup).

asi.hypothesis: Re-establish current floor after resume
asi.result: 5 runs: 0.89/0.90/0.91/0.94/0.94. Best 0.89s avg ~0.92s, consistent with prior session.

asi.hypothesis: pool=vmForks for src
asi.result: Slower (1.08 vs 0.89)

description: Collapse src+worker projects into single vitest config

asi.hypothesis: Collapse src+worker projects into single top-level vitest config (one worker pool of 8 threads instead of two pools of 8). Single env shared.
asi.result: 0.89 -> 0.80 best (3 runs 0.80/0.81/0.84). 1404 tests pass; coverage unchanged: lines 99.34%, branches 97.69%, functions 97.6%, statements 99%.
asi.key_insight: vitest projects each spawn their own worker pool; collapsing reduces total worker boot overhead by 50%.

asi.hypothesis: maxConcurrency=20 for concurrent describe blocks
asi.result: 0.80 -> 0.79 (within noise). Slight gain at best.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=4 with single-project (after collapse)
asi.result: 8 tests fail. Same isolate=false + reduced workers state pollution issue.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers/minWorkers=12 to spread import load across more threads (more parallelism)
asi.result: 0.79 -> 0.77 (3 runs 0.82/0.77/0.79). All 1404 tests pass; coverage unchanged. Slight gain.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=12 only (drop minWorkers - not supported in vitest 4 top-level)
asi.result: 0.80/0.79/0.77 - same gain as before. typecheck passes.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=16
asi.result: 0.79-0.86, slightly worse than 12 (0.77 best). Diminishing returns past 12.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=10
optimizeDeps.include hot deps
asi.result: Slightly slower (0.82-0.83 vs 0.77-0.80)
asi.rollback_reason: No gain (likely vitest doesn't use vite optimizeDeps in test transformPipeline)

asi.hypothesis: deps.inline=true (force inline transform of all deps)
asi.result: Much slower (1.3s+); inlining all node_modules adds transform cost.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=14
asi.result: 0.79-0.85, slightly worse than 12

asi.hypothesis: excludeAfterRemap=true (skip exclude-matching files after remap)
asi.result: 0.78-0.84, within noise of 0.77-0.80

asi.hypothesis: Explicit cacheDir (default already same)
asi.result: Same. No change

asi.hypothesis: Final stability check after Round 3 (collapse projects + maxWorkers=12 + maxConcurrency=20)
asi.result: 5 runs: 0.79-0.85, avg ~0.82s. -56% from baseline. Documentation updated.

asi.hypothesis: Re-establish floor on resume
asi.result: 5 runs: 0.80-0.86, best 0.80s. Floor consistent with prior session.

asi.hypothesis: esbuild target=esnext to skip downlevel transforms
asi.result: 0.82-0.83 (within noise)

asi.hypothesis: Disable Vite preTransformRequests
asi.result: Within noise (0.81-0.90)

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=8 (default) explicitly
asi.result: 0.81-0.87, slightly worse than 12

asi.hypothesis: Try SWC transformer via unplugin-swc
asi.result: 0.86-0.92, slightly slower than esbuild. SWC overhead dominates over single-file transform speed advantage.

asi.hypothesis: sequence.concurrent=true (force all tests concurrent)
asi.result: Many tests fail (mocks shared between concurrent tests in same file).

asi.hypothesis: Verify floor unchanged after experiments
asi.result: 5 runs: 0.81-0.90 (best 0.81). Avg 0.85. Floor confirmed.

asi.hypothesis: execArgv=['--no-warnings'] for thread workers
asi.result: Within noise (0.81-0.87)

description: Stub next/server alias (no clear win, risk)

asi.hypothesis: Alias next/server to a tiny stub to skip 16MB Next.js loading
asi.result: 0.80-0.97 (avg 0.83). Within noise of floor (0.81 best). Marginal improvement at best.
asi.rollback_reason: Marginal gain not worth the risk: aliasing next/server globally in tests could mask production behavior differences (NextResponse.json content-type ordering, NextRequest.cookies, etc). Only 3 test files import it; mostly already mocked.

asi.hypothesis: Update ideas backlog with Round 3 outcomes (no code change)
asi.result: Floor confirmed at ~0.81s. Ideas pruned, gotchas documented.

asi.hypothesis: silent=passed-only to suppress stdout from passing tests
asi.result: 0.82-0.83 (within noise)

asi.hypothesis: chaiConfig.includeStack=false, truncateThreshold lower
asi.result: Within noise (0.82-0.85)

asi.hypothesis: Long stability sweep (7 runs) on resume
asi.result: 0.81-0.85, median 0.81. Floor solid.

asi.hypothesis: Custom largest-first test sequencer to schedule heaviest files first (improve worker load balance with isolate=false)
asi.result: 0.81-0.85 (median 0.83). Same as default sequencer.
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable gain. Default vitest sequencer already balances load adequately on 12 workers / 70 files. Cleanup unused file.

name: Refactor: reduce file/function complexity while maintaining coverage 95%+

description: Baseline: 13 files over 400 lines, max 869 (post.ts), coverage 98.61%/96.44%/96.99%

asi.hypothesis: establish baseline for complexity refactoring

description: Split post.ts (869) into 7 modules + barrel; decompose updatePost

asi.hypothesis: split post.ts (869 LOC) into 7 cohesive modules (types/cache/queries/mutations/tags/aggregates/search) + barrel re-export. updatePost is now decomposed into 5 helpers (applySimpleFields, applyContentUpdate, applyExcerptUpdate, applyReferenceUpdate, applyPublishedAt) each <30 LOC.

description: Split admin-posts-client.tsx (849) into 6 cohesive component files

asi.hypothesis: split admin-posts-client.tsx (849) into 6 components: sortable-header, copy-link, pagination, bulk-action-bar, list-view, grid-view + main container. Top-level component now <200 LOC; list-view further decomposed into PostTableHeader/PostRow/PostRowTags/PostRowActions sub-components each <80 LOC.

description: Split analytics.ts (785) into 6 modules; dedupe search/ai bot detail via shared loader

asi.hypothesis: split analytics.ts (785) into 6 modules: helpers (time windows/source conditions/gap fills/title resolution), record (ingest), overview (summary queries), human, bot-details (search+ai share loader), other. Extract `loadBotDetail` helper to dedupe search vs ai-bot detail queries; extract `normalizeCounts` + `pct` from overview.

description: Split unfurl.ts (468) into 6 modules; decompose fetchHtml + extractOgMetadata helpers

asi.hypothesis: split unfurl.ts (468) into 6 modules: types (UnfurlError), ssrf (validateUrl/resolveAndValidateHostname), fetch (fetchHtml + readBodyWithCap + fetchHopRaw helpers), extract (OG meta + body text + matchMetaContent helper), github (README image + helpers), main barrel. fetchHtml decomposed into readBodyWithCap + fetchHopRaw; extractOgMetadata uses shared matchMetaContent.

description: Split media-library.tsx (679) into 6 modules; extract useMediaUpload hook

asi.hypothesis: split media-library.tsx (679) into 6 modules: helpers (FileTypeIcon/buildQuery/hasActiveFilters/formatDate/MediaWithUrl), filter-bar, grid (+ MediaGridCard sub), preview lightbox, upload hook (useMediaUpload), main container. Extracts drag-and-drop into reusable hook.

description: Split mcp-tokens-manager.tsx (546) into setup-guide + table + manager

asi.hypothesis: split mcp-tokens-manager.tsx (546) into 3 modules: setup-guide (CopyButton/CodeBlock/McpSetupGuide ~165 lines), table (McpTokensTable/TokenRow/ScopeCell/formatDate/statusFor ~150 lines), manager (~272 lines). Extracts shared callApi helper for fetch+JSON+errorMsg.

description: Split backup-page.tsx (524) into helpers + push-card + pull-card

asi.hypothesis: split backup-page.tsx (524) into 4 modules: helpers (StatusMessage/BackupCopyButton/HistoryRow), pull-card (self-contained with own state), push-card (Push state + PushDetailBox + HistorySection sub-components), main barrel (20 lines).

description: Split site-identity-form.tsx (471) into logo + social + form

asi.hypothesis: split site-identity-form.tsx (471) into 3 components: site-logo-card (drag-and-drop logo upload + state, ~175 lines), social-links-card (SocialLinkRow + add/remove logic, ~120 lines), site-identity-form (main settings form, ~200 lines). Coverage retained at 98.65%/96.41%.

description: Split system-monitor-dashboard.tsx (454) into helpers + chart + dashboard

asi.hypothesis: split system-monitor-dashboard.tsx (454) into 3 modules: helpers (formatters + StatCard + MemoryRecommendation + LoadingSkeleton + types + CHART_COLORS), chart (MemoryTrendChart with downsample helper), dashboard (~115 lines, fetch + composition).

description: Split ai-agents-manager.tsx (452) into helpers + table + manager

asi.hypothesis: split ai-agents-manager.tsx (452) into 3 modules: helpers (CopyButton/DeleteAgentDialog/NewAgentModal + AgentWithAvatarUrl type), table (AgentRow + AiAgentsTable), manager (~165 lines, fetch+state+modals). Extracts fetchAgentPrompt helper that dedupes new-agent vs show-prompt fallback logic.
